entries.notes: Runner evidence authority lands with baseline and mutant runner work.
Candidate determinism requires AST candidate ordering and mutant model tests.
Canonical report ordering lands with report renderer snapshots.
AI boundary tests land with stub provider and context schema work.
Backend default enforcement is first covered by config and version-policy tests.
Pinned Zig 0.16.0 checks land with Zig version policy.
No permanent worktree mutation is covered by sandbox tests.
Original-text span validation is covered by patch sandbox tests.
Test declaration exclusion is covered by AST traversal fixtures.
Compile-error classification lands with mutant runner tests.
Invalid mutant semantics start with the shared mutant model and continue through sandbox tests.
Test selection reporting is covered when selection strategies exist.
Cache-key input coverage lands with cache-key design tests.
Task and schema registry versions are validated by the bootstrap validator; report and AI schema contract tests land later.
Snapshot normalization lands with report and doctest snapshots.
Doctest deterministic case IDs land with extraction tests.
The validator enforces at most one active pending-completion task and rejects implemented/verified task-control states.
The validator checks Markdown and JSON task queue/status consistency.
Pipeline artifact role timestamps will prove failing evidence was recorded before implementation.
The validator rejects prose-only follow-up bullets unless they explicitly state no predefined follow-up exists, and validates concrete task references in task Markdown.
Compiler crash classification lands with mutant runner tests.
The validator requires ADR-0008 architecture-boundary contracts and checks source layer declarations plus deterministic-core import boundaries once src/**/*.zig files exist.
